title: lithocarpus leiocarpus
intro: Lithocarpus leiocarpus, also known as the Bur Oak, is a deciduous tree species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The Bur Oak is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional folk medicine, where its bark, leaves, and acorns are used to treat various ailments. The plant has been used to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and even as a treatment for wounds. Lithocarpus leiocarpus can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, making it a valuable resource for those seeking natural remedies.
